articles.title: Total repression and air strikes bring daily unrelenting dread for Iranians
articles.body: "I cannot even remember how I used to live in the past without being reminded of the loved one I lost during the protests," she says. "I fear tomorrow. I fear the person I will be tomorrow. Today, I survive somehow, but how will I get through tomorrow? That is the real question. Will I even live through tomorrow?"

articles.title: Death of Ali Larijani deepens crisis at heart of Iran's leadership
articles.body: His removal leaves these issues unresolved and transfers them to an as-yet-unknown successor facing an extremely fragile situation. While Iran has shown resilience, partly by disrupting global energy markets, its airspace remains open to continued strikes. Any new senior figure will face immediate risk of being targeted.

articles.title: Top US counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigns over Iran war
articles.body: Kent, 45, is a US special forces and CIA veteran whose wife, navy cryptologic technician Shannon Kent, was killed in a suicide bombing in Syria in 2019.

articles.title: Ozempic, Wegovy: India's cheap weight-loss drugs could reshape global obesity fight
articles.body: Several Indian drugmakers are already preparing to make the move. According to Sheetal Sapale, vice-president at research firm Pharmarack, major firms including Cipla, Sun Pharma, Dr Reddy's Laboratories, Biocon, Natco, Zydus and Mankind Pharma are readying branded generics, with many more likely to follow. Prices are expected to fall sharply.

articles.body:
Juliana Stratton wins Illinois Democratic primary for US Senate

The 60-year-old Democrat, backed by Governor JB Pritzker, pulled off a surprise victory after months of trailing in the polls.

articles.title: Italy warns Russian tanker Arctic Metagaz could explode in Mediterranean

articles.title: US Attorney General Pam Bondi formally summoned to Congress in Epstein case
articles.body: "As Attorney General, you are directly responsible for overseeing the Department's collection, review, and determinations regarding the release of files pursuant to the Epstein Files Transparency Act, and the Committee therefore believes that you possess valuable insight into these efforts," he said of Bondi. Bondi has been asked to appear on 14 April.

articles.title: Water supply cut in Moldova after oil spill blamed on Russian strike in Ukraine
articles.body: Moldova has a population of 2.4 million, but Russia still has a military base in the Russian-speaking, breakaway region of Transnistria, which lies along much of Moldova's eastern border with Ukraine. Local TV reports say oil has been spotted in Transnistria although officials there do not expect to impose restrictions on drinking water.

articles.title: Chile's president begins building border barrier less than week into term
articles.body: Echoing his rhetoric on the campaign trail, Kast said Chile had been "violated by illegal immigration, drug trafficking and organised crime", adding that he wanted to used the diggers behind him to "build a sovereign Chile".

articles.title: Michael B Jordan marks Oscars win with In-N-Out burger
articles.body: Celebrities are a common sight at the popular US fast food chain, perhaps in part due to its relationship with the Oscars - Vanity Fair, which hosts the now-legendary afterparty, has served In-N-Out burgers at the event since 1994, according to US media.

articles.title: Sri Lanka declares all Wednesdays off to conserve energy
articles.body: In Thailand, for example, the government is urging people to swap suits for short-sleeved tees to reduce reliance on air conditioning, while in Myanmar, private vehicles are allowed only to operate on alternates days depending on their licence plate numbers.

articles.body: Although Khamenei's son, Mojtaba, was chosen as his successor, he has not been seen in public or appeared in any recent photographs or videos since the start of the war, having reportedly been injured in the Israeli strike that killed his father.

articles.title: LNG: Will the Iran war squeeze India's piped gas next?

The government has given priority to these two sectors," says Rahul Chopra, managing director for the Haryana City Gas Distribution Limited, a countrywide gas company with around 100,000 domestic consumers and 195 CNG fuel stations.

articles.title: 'Fire came from the sky' - life on the brink of civil war in South Sudan

articles.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
articles.body: In an environment so full of possible dangers, it is not surprising that countries are hesitating to get involved, preferring instead to call, as the UK government has, for de-escalation as the surest way of unlocking the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: 'My mother cried out one last time': Palestinian boy, 12, describes how Israeli forces killed his family in car
articles.body: Between 7 October 2023 and 15 March 2026, the UN's humanitarian affairs office, OCHA, says 1,071 Palestinians were killed in the West Bank, including at least 233 children.

articles.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
articles.body: Restricting internet access not only restricts communication with the outside world, but also limits protesters' ability to mobilise, plan and communicate among each other. Encrypted messaging apps and platforms often function as tools for organising rallies, sharing protest locations, and circulating calls to action.

articles.title: Why did only two Iranian football players stay in Australia?
articles.body:
After Australia granted seven Iranian football players humanitarian visas to stay in the country, only two have decided to stay.

According to human rights activists, those who left feared reprisals from the Iranian Government, but the semi-official Mehr news agency called it a "patriotic decision".

The footballers had originally sought sanctuary after concerns they would face repercussions for staying silent during the country's anthem at their opening Asian Cup match.

articles.title: Woman is steps away from lava as volcano erupts on Reunion Island
articles.body:
Tourists and locals have been gathered on the Reunion Island to see lava flowing from a volcano that has been erupting for over a month.

On Sunday, lava from the Piton de la Fournaise volcano reached the Indian ocean for the first time in 19 years.

The volcano began erupting 13 February, for the second time this year. Two lava flows from the volcano have since caused disruption after cutting the national highway linking the south and east of the island.

articles.title: Watch the top moments from the 2026 Oscars
articles.body:
The 2026 Oscars featured surprise wins, emotional speeches and some comical moments as stars gathered for Hollywood's biggest night.

The ceremony saw action thriller One Battle After Another win the most Oscars of the night with six wins, including best picture. But there were a number of historic moments - including Jessie Buckley becoming the first Irish star to win best actress.

And in a shock win amid tough competition from Leonardo di Caprio and Timothée Chalamet, Michael B Jordan took home the best actor prize - the sixth black actor to win in the Academy's nearly 100-year history.

Follow the BBC's ongoing coverage of the Oscars here.

articles.title: Mexico City breaks Guinness Record with giant football training session
articles.body:
A sea of people took over Mexico City's Zocalo square on Sunday, breaking the Guinness World Records for largest football lesson.

The city's main square became an open-air training ground with 9,500 people practicing football drills under the guidance of instructors.

Organisers said the turnout surpassed the previous Guinness World Records set in Seattle in June 2025, when a football class brought together over 1,000 participants.

The attempt comes as the country prepares to host the opening match of the FIFA World Cup in June.
